milestone: World Builder -- Delete a Personal Theme everywhere, not just locally
slice: 'i deleted it twice but it keeps coming back' -- a real, confirmed bug on the Welcome screen's My World Projects list. It re-checks Supabase on every render and shows a card for any Personal/Official Theme this session owns even with no local draft; Delete only ever removed the LOCAL draft, deliberately, to make an Official theme un-nukeable. Applied to a Personal theme the author owns, that same rule meant Delete could never finish -- the Personal Repository row and Cloud Backup both stayed real, so the next render redrew the identical card. The exact card this produces (a Personal theme with no local draft, _repoOnlyCard) also had no delete control at all -- clicking it only ever restored/cloned it. Fixed: the shared js/themeRepositoryClient.js gained deleteTheme(repositoryId, themeId) (mirrors reset()'s real-delete-confirmation discipline -- Storage removed before the row, .select() chained after .delete() -- scoped to one theme_id instead of a whole repository); js/services/projectSync.js gained remove(projectId) (deletes one Cloud Backup row, owner-scoped). worldBuilderApp.js's local-card Delete now calls both, best-effort, after removing the local draft immediately; its confirm dialog discloses this. _repoOnlyCard gained a real Delete button for kind==='personal' only -- never for 'official', by construction. Verified via a new 2-scenario Playwright test: a repo-only Personal card with a Cloud Backup (the exact reported shape) and a real local card whose World is also Personal-published, both confirmed via the mocks' own call logs. Full regression across 16 prior scripts + 2 new scenarios + both goldenBuild.js suites unchanged.
